data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETF Holdings Experience Significant Reduction Since Last October
data.description: Bitcoin spot ETF holdings in the United States have seen a substantial decrease since reaching an all-time high last October. According to Foresight News, the holdings have reduced by approximately 100,300 BTC during this cycle. This reduction is attributed to institutional investors' risk-averse strategies, which have intensified the market's weakness and reinforced the prevailing risk-averse sentiment, exerting structural pressure on Bitcoin's price.

data.title: BGD Labs to cease Aave contributions after four years as governance tensions grow
data.description:
BGD Labs said it will cease contributions to Aave after April 1, ending a four-year role as a core protocol contributor.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/390629/bgd-labs-to-cease-aave-contributions-after-four-years-as-governance-tensions-grow?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: OpenAI Launches EVMbench to Enhance Smart Contract Security
data.description: OpenAI has unveiled EVMbench, a benchmark aimed at evaluating AI models' capabilities in identifying, fixing, and exploiting vulnerabilities in smart contracts. According to NS3.AI, this initiative underscores the growing significance of comprehending smart contracts as AI agents may evolve into autonomous entities within crypto-native settings. The benchmark signifies progress towards incorporating AI-driven autonomous operations in blockchain ecosystems, with potential impacts on security and the infrastructure of decentralized economies.

data.title: Trump: Government shutdown caused at least 2 percentage points of loss to US GDP.
data.description: Walter Bloomberg wrote on the X platform that Trump said the government shutdown caused the US GDP to lose at least 2 percentage points.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Brazilian Company Challenges Equinox Gold's Asset Sale to Chinese Miner
data.description: A Brazilian state-owned enterprise has initiated legal proceedings to prevent the sale of a precious metals asset by Equinox Gold to a major Chinese mining company. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing dispute over the transaction, which involves significant interests in the mining sector. The Brazilian company is seeking to halt the deal, citing concerns over the implications of transferring ownership to a foreign entity. This legal challenge underscores the complexities and geopolitical considerations in international mining operations, particularly involving assets of strategic importance. The outcome of this case could have broader ramifications for future transactions in the industry.

data.title: German Chancellor Merz Advocates for Flexible Work Schedules
data.description: German Chancellor Merz has expressed skepticism about the effectiveness of a four-day workweek in boosting productivity. According to Jin10, Merz emphasized the need for flexibility in work arrangements and the importance of rewarding individuals who are willing to put in extra effort. He also highlighted the necessity of facilitating the return to work for employees. Merz's comments reflect a broader discussion on work-life balance and productivity in Germany.

data.title: Polymarket data shows the market expects a 94% probability that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates unchanged in March.
data.description: According to Polymarket, a forecasting market platform, as of March 18, the probability of the Federal Reserve maintaining interest rates unchanged at its March meeting was 94%, a significant increase from previous levels; the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut was about 5%, a rate cut of more than 50 basis points was less than 1%, and a rate hike of more than 25 basis points was also about 1%. The cumulative trading volume for this contract was approximately $144 million. Market pricing reflects that traders generally expect policy to remain stable in the short term.

data.title: Wall Street's Focus on Akamai, Grail, Hudbay, Newmont, and Opendoor
data.description: Wall Street is closely monitoring several companies ahead of the market opening. Bloomberg posted on X that Akamai Technologies is drawing attention due to its recent strategic moves. Grail, a biotechnology firm, is also in the spotlight following developments in its cancer detection technology. Hudbay Minerals and Newmont Corporation, both significant players in the mining sector, are being watched for their latest operational updates and market performance. Additionally, Opendoor Technologies, known for its real estate transactions, is under scrutiny as investors assess its business strategies and market impact. These companies are expected to influence market trends as trading begins.

data.title: ‘Bitcoin Going to Zero’ Searches Hit 5-Year High as Market Braces for Shift
data.description:
Searches for “Bitcoin going to zero” just hit a five-year high on Google Trends. The spike showed up in early 2026, matching a stretch of choppy price action and rising volatility. The jump matters because retail search behavior often tracks fear, and fear tends to shape short-term market moves
source: https://techgaged.com/bitcoin-going-to-zero-searches-hit-5-year-high-as-market-braces-for-shift/

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iran's Foreign Minister Emphasizes Diplomacy for Nuclear Program Resolution
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi stated on February 20 that the country's peaceful nuclear program cannot be resolved through military means, emphasizing that diplomacy is the only viable solution. According to Jin10, Araghchi highlighted past attempts to disrupt Iran's nuclear efforts, including large-scale attacks on facilities and assassinations of scientists, which have failed to dismantle the program. He noted that the technology was developed indigenously, making it impervious to destruction through bombing or military action. Consequently, the United States has returned to the negotiating table to seek an agreement. Araghchi affirmed Iran's desire for peace and its willingness to resolve issues through negotiation and diplomacy, while also being prepared to defend its security.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Criticizes UK's Plan to Transfer Chagos Islands to Mauritius
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed disapproval of the United Kingdom's decision to transfer the Chagos Islands' ownership to Mauritius. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic significance of the islands, which have been a point of contention between the UK and Mauritius for decades. The Chagos Islands, located in the Indian Ocean, are home to a key military base used by both the UK and the United States. The transfer plan has sparked international debate, with concerns over regional security and geopolitical implications. The UK government has faced pressure from the United Nations and other international bodies to return the islands to Mauritius, following a 2019 International Court of Justice advisory opinion. The decision has drawn mixed reactions, with some supporting the move as a step towards decolonization, while others, including President Trump, view it as a potential threat to strategic interests in the region.

data.title: Trump claims the government shutdown is damaging the economy, reiterated his call for interest rate cuts, and criticized Powell.
data.description: US President Trump posted on the Truth platform that the government shutdown initiated by the Democrats caused a drop of at least two percentage points in US GDP. That's why they're now repeating the same trick on a smaller scale. Stop the government shutdown! Lower interest rates. Powell is the worst.

data.title: Turkish Journalist Alican Uludag Arrested for Insulting President Erdogan
data.description: A Turkish court has officially detained journalist Alican Uludag on charges of insulting President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing tensions between the Turkish government and media professionals. The arrest is part of a broader crackdown on dissent and freedom of expression in Turkey, where numerous journalists have faced legal action for their reporting and commentary. This development underscores the challenges faced by media personnel in the country, as they navigate a landscape marked by stringent government oversight and restrictions on press freedom.

data.title: S&P Considers High-Grade Rating for EA Bonds
data.description: S&P Global Ratings is reportedly considering assigning a high-grade rating to $1.5 billion worth of Electronic Arts (EA) bonds. Bloomberg posted on X that this potential move could prevent debt investors from accessing a lucrative buyout premium. The decision by S&P could significantly impact the financial strategies of investors involved with EA bonds. The high-grade rating would indicate a lower risk of default, potentially affecting the bonds' attractiveness in the market. This development comes as investors closely monitor the credit ratings of major corporations, which can influence investment decisions and market dynamics.

data.title: CryptoQuant: Binance Controls 65% of Exchange Stablecoin Liquidity as Capital Concentrates
data.description: Binance is emerging as the dominant venue for crypto liquidity as stablecoin balances and spot trading activity increasingly concentrate on a single exchange, according to new data from CryptoQuant.CryptoQuant data shows that $47.5 billion in stablecoins is currently held on one exchange, with Binance accounting for roughly 65% of all stablecoin liquidity across centralized exchanges. The concentration comes even as broader bear market outflows continue to slow, signaling a shift in where capital is parked rather than an exit from crypto altogether.Stablecoin Liquidity Concentrates on BinanceStablecoins are a key proxy for deployable capital in crypto markets, often used as dry powder for trading, hedging, and risk management. CryptoQuant’s figures indicate that while overall exchange balances have stabilized, liquidity is increasingly clustering on Binance rather than dispersing across competitors.This dynamic suggests that traders and institutions are prioritizing venues with deeper order books, higher execution reliability, and lower slippage during volatile conditions.Binance Drives January Spot Market GrowthSpot market data reinforces the same trend. In January, Binance generated $409 billion in spot trading volume, marking a 12% month-over-month increase, according to CryptoQuant. That figure accounted for nearly half of total global centralized exchange (CEX) spot market growth during the month.By comparison, Binance’s spot volume was almost five times larger than the next-largest exchange, underscoring a widening gap in effective liquidity.The data points to real trading activity rather than promotional volume, with CryptoQuant highlighting that the growth reflects sustained user participation and capital deployment.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | International Spot Gold Shows Potential for Continued Rise
data.description: International spot gold is gradually raising its bottom, moving away from the lower range. According to Jin10, it is stabilizing at the upper edge of the dense chip area around 50XX, indicating a potential for continued upward movement.

data.title: Michael Saylor: If BTC doesn't go to zero, it will reach $1 million.
data.description: Michael Saylor posted on the X platform that if BTC does not go to zero, its price will reach $1 million.

data.title: Income Tax Refunds Show 15% Year-on-Year Increase in 2023
data.description: Income tax refunds issued this year have increased by 15% compared to the previous year. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X. This growth reflects a significant rise in cumulative refunds when compared to prior years, dating back to 2023. The data highlights a notable trend in the financial landscape, indicating changes in tax refund patterns.

data.title: Stablecoin Market Value Faces Significant Decline
data.description: The market value of the world's largest stablecoin is experiencing a significant decline this month. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the notable pullback in the stablecoin's valuation. This development comes amid broader market fluctuations and economic uncertainties affecting the cryptocurrency sector. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation as it unfolds, considering the potential implications for the digital currency market. The decline in the stablecoin's market value reflects ongoing volatility and investor sentiment in the financial landscape.

data.title: South African Banks Secure $322 Million in Loss-Absorbing Debt
data.description: South Africa's largest banks have successfully raised $322 million in loss-absorbing debt to comply with new regulations designed to prevent taxpayer-funded bailouts. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this financial maneuver as part of efforts to strengthen the banking sector's resilience. The move comes in response to regulatory changes that require banks to hold additional capital to absorb losses during financial distress, thereby reducing the risk of government intervention. This development marks a proactive step by South African banks to align with international standards and safeguard the financial system against potential crises.
